/privacy.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/privacy.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
p: I take the protection of your personal data seriously and treat it confidentially & in accordance with applicable data protection laws, especially the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
This privacy policy explains what data is collected when you visit this site and how it is used.
When visiting this website, your browser automatically sends the following data, which may be stored in server log files:
IP address (anonymized if possible) • Date and time of request • Referring URL • Browser type and version • Operating system
This data is used solely for technical monitoring and security. It is not combined with other data sources.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in secure website operation)
If you contact me via email or contact form, the data you provide (name, email, message content) will be stored and processed for the purpose of responding to your inquiry.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (pre-contractual measures and communication)
This website may use cookies — small text files stored on your device — to enhance functionality or analyze usage. You can control or disable cookies in your browser settings.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent, where applicable) and/or Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in website usability)

This website is hosted by Framer. Personal data collected on this website is stored on the servers of the hosting provider. The provider is bound by data protection agreements (Data Processing Agreement – DPA).

• Request info about the data I store about you (Art. 15 GDPR)• Request correction or deletion of your data (Art. 16 & 17 GDPR)• Restrict or object to processing (Art. 18 & 21 GDPR)• Data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)• Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority
To exercise your rights, please contact me at hello@ben-e.me.

• Request info on data stored about you (Art. 15 GDPR)• Request correction/deletion of your data (Art. 16 & 17 GDPR)• Restrict or object to processing (Art. 18 & 21 GDPR)• Data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)• Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority
• Request info about the data I store about you• Request correction or deletion of your data• Restrict or object to processing • Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority• Data portability
I use technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against accidental or intentional manipulation, loss, destruction, or unauthorized access.
I may update this privacy policy from time to time to reflect legal or technical changes. Please check back occasionally.

/tos.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/tos.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
p: These Terms and Conditions apply to all offers, services, and contracts between the service provider (hereinafter referred to as the “Service Provider”) and the client. Deviating terms are only valid if confirmed in writing by the Service Provider.
The agreed scope of services is defined in the written offer. Changes or additions must be confirmed in writing.

The client shall provide all required information, materials, and content necessary for execution in a timely manner. Delays caused by missing input from the client will extend delivery timelines accordingly.

Unless otherwise agreed, one revision round per deliverable is included. Additional changes will be charged separately based on time spent. Final approval must be provided in writing (e.g., via email).

All prices are stated net, plus applicable VAT. Invoices are due within 14 days unless otherwise agreed. The Service Provider may request an upfront partial payment of up to 50 % of the total project amount before work begins.

Usage rights for the delivered work are transferred to the client only after full payment. Unless otherwise agreed, the client is granted a non-exclusive right of use limited to the agreed purpose. Any further use or transfer requires written permission from the Service Provider.
The Service Provider is liable only in cases of willful misconduct or gross negligence. The client is solely responsible for the legality and content of all materials provided.
If a project is canceled by the client, the Service Provider is entitled to compensation for all services rendered up to that point. Advance payments are non-refundable.
German law shall apply. The place of jurisdiction is the Service Prodiver’s business location, provided the client is a registered business entity.

Diese allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen (AGB) gelten für alle Angebote, Leistungen und Verträge zwischen dem Auftragnehmer (nachfolgend „Dienstleister“) und dem Auftraggeber. Abweichende Bedingungen des Auftraggebers gelten nur, wenn sie schriftlich bestätigt wurden.
Der Leistungsumfang ergibt sich aus dem schriftlich vereinbarten Angebot. Änderungen oder Erweiterungen bedürfen der schriftlichen Bestätigung.

Der Auftraggeber stellt alle zur Ausführung notwendigen Informationen, Materialien und Inhalte rechtzeitig zur Verfügung. Verzögerungen, die durch fehlende Mitwirkung entstehen, verlängern die vereinbarten Fristen entsprechend.

Sofern nicht anders vereinbart, ist eine Korrekturschleife pro Leistung enthalten. Weitere Änderungen werden nach Aufwand gesondert abgerechnet. Die finale Freigabe erfolgt schriftlich (z. B. per E-Mail).

Alle Preise verstehen sich netto zzgl. gesetzlicher Umsatzsteuer. Rechnungen sind, sofern nicht anders angegeben, innerhalb von 14 Tagen ohne Abzug zahlbar. Bei Projektbeginn kann eine Abschlagszahlung von bis zu 50 % des Gesamtbetrags vereinbart werden.

Die Nutzungsrechte an den erbrachten Leistungen gehen erst nach vollständiger Bezahlung auf den Auftraggeber über. Sofern nicht anders vereinbart, erhält der Auftraggeber ein einfaches Nutzungsrecht zur vereinbarten Verwendung. Eine Weitergabe oder  Verwendung über das Projekt hinaus bedarf der Zustimmung des Dienstleisters.
Der Dienstleister haftet nur für Vorsatz und grobe Fahrlässigkeit. Für Inhalte und rechtliche Zulässigkeit bereitgestellter Materialien ist der Auftraggeber verantwortlich.
Wird ein Projekt vom Auftraggeber abgebrochen, sind alle bis dahin erbrachten Leistungen anteilig zu vergüten. Bereits geleistete Zahlungen werden nicht erstattet.
Es gilt deutsches Recht. Gerichtsstand ist der Sitz des Dienstleisters, sofern der Auftraggeber Kaufmann im Sinne des HGB ist.

/imprint.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/imprint.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
p: The European Commission provides an online dispute resolution platform:https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr
For information on how personal data is handled, please refer to the Privacy Policy.
I am not obligated and not willing to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

All content on this website is created by me unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction, editing, or distribution beyond personal, non-commercial use requires prior written permission. Third-party content is clearly marked and respected. If you notice any copyright issues, please contact me — such content will be removed promptly.

The content on this website has been created with great care. However, I cannot guarantee that all information is complete, accurate, or up to date. As a service provider, I am responsible for my own content according to § 7 para. 1 TMG. I am not obliged to monitor external information transmitted or stored, or to investigate circumstances indicating illegal activity (§§ 8–10 TMG).

This website may contain links to external websites. I have no control over the content of those sites and accept no liability for them. The linked pages were checked at the time of linking for possible legal violations; unlawful content was not detected. Permanent monitoring of linked pages is unreasonable without concrete evidence of a violation. Upon notification of such violations, I will remove those links immediately.

/cs-zack.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/cs-zack.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
p: Dojo Madness is a gaming startup harnessing big data to build tools & services that help players to master their favorite games.
Cara Care develops a SaaS medical App to improve the quality of life for people with digestive issues like IBS, IBD and others.
Conducted job interviews, hired team members
Design Team organization, planning & process optimization
Launched product from ideation to v1.1
End-to-end design process for web- & desktop app
Actively shaped product roadmap and feature set
Creation & maintenance of Atom Design System
Support for marketing, sales & content via print, merchandise, web & motion design
Support for marketing, sales & content via print, merch, web & motion
Over the timespan of roughly a year, Zack.ai managed to go from the first ideation phase past MVP and beta testing to a regularly iterated live product loved by millions of users.

It not only continued with useful features where the company’s previous vertical products for specific esports games ended but also pulled the separate user groups together with a more holistic approach, maximizing usage time by covering all game-phases and a broad catalog of supported games.

After the Company had launched several successful vertical products covering the depths of top played competitive games at the time, it became clear that the effort to identify & create the next product in line was likely not profitable enough, despite the brand, design system & basic feature set from the existing products.

Since the freemium monetisation model of the existing verticals was doing well enough but was still only purchased by a small fraction of the separated user-groups, the need for a more holistic approach was clear — unifying the user-bases, maximising usage time & ditching the freemium model for a big-data approach.

In conclusion the goal was to not only cover a way wider catalog of supported games but also making sure the product covered as many needs around the process of gaming as possible in order to gather the maximum of users & data to analise, utilise and monetise later on.
Since this was a "companies first" without a clear indicator of profitability — a closely watched and iterated MVP & Beta approach was the option of choice.
From separate Verticals to a unifying Approach
informed Decisions towards Product Market Fit
Fortunately there were a lot of insights & learnings gathered over the previous years of vertical product coverage the team was able to benefit from:
detailed game data is the main driver for useful coaching features but is usually very rare to be accessible.
in-game support is the most liked user-feature (but hardest to balance in terms of live data availability and "cheating" accusations)
analytical (post game) features have the highest learning value for users but a detailed analysis demands a sophisticated algorithm and constant rebalancing regarding meta-shifts etc
the lions share of users focusses on a rough performance evaluation instead of a detailed analysis with improvement markers
With this foundation of insights the MVP focussed on the following two main drivers for product success:
Maximise the user group by supporting as many games as possible
various support levels per game (Base support: News, patch-notes, selected Youtube clips • Advanced: basic in-game data available for basic analytics • Top Tier: live-features and detailed reports)
gradual feature roll-out and game catalogue expansion based on a games playerbase, data availability & user feedback
Maximise the product usage to gather detailed usage data
combine general per-game news with in-game support & post-game analytics for a repetitive full game cycle support
news-feed approach for beneficial content consumption esp. in pre- and post-game and minimized feature card experiments
constant product iteration & feature card experiments based on usage data and on-card feedback options
bring the "AI-assistant" approach to life with smart user interactions and micro-animations

Zack.ai is an intelligent gaming assistant that enables the user to focus on what matters most - enjoy gaming! He collects various news about the users favorite games from around the web, decides on what matters most for the specific user based on his preferences and delivers it in easily digestible bits.

A big goal from the very beginning was to really bring Zack to life and give him a distinct character so that the assistant experience would feel as real as possible — like a personal conversation whenever the user interacts with him.

This was the perfect place to integrate some well crafted micro animations even though they were mainly attached to a speech bubble in the early and fast paced stages of the product. The difference in the characters perception really felt like an upgrade.

We experimented early on with a more assistant-guided user experience to shape the platform just as much as the assistant brand itself. The early alpha version of the onboarding below demonstrates this vision quite nicely.
From News-Feed to rich interactions & platform specific use-case split
From News-Feed to platform specific use-case split

To satisfy the main goal of small and compact bits of news, a feed view with smart cards was the clear choice for both visual & information architecture reasons. In pursuit of a perpetually minimalistic & clear interface, constant iterations of both the card UI & UX were necessary. Content became richer, interactive and more enjoyable to consume.

One of the biggest factors that enabled us to constantly iterate & improve the product experience was a clearly structured & flexible atom design system. Cleverly nested symbols/components, clear naming conventions and a design library as a visual representation of the atom structure were our best friends.

To develop even better coverage of all the 3 game phases, we created a separate mobile-app and focussed on the separation of platform-specific use cases to generate more depth of information only where needed.

The mobile news-feed became focus for a maximized ease-of-use & fast consumption, while detailed strategies & game analysis received a more prominent role on dedicated per-game dashboards for the desktop client.
Approaching users from various angles & communication styles

Even the marketing efforts were constantly revamped on the basis of user feedback and various KPIs from our in-house analytics team. The landing page evolution a great example — it went from a minimalistic signup page in the alpha-phase to a detailed description of the product USP and introduction to our platforms including desktop & mobile.

The complexity of the markets prescription / reimbursement system had to be tackled through a well structured and extensively guiding customer journey that enables multiple entry points (doctor referrals, self-managed, etc.) and confidently guides the user with supportively chunked steps, reminders  & info material.

In addition also we picked up on our marketing-learnings from previous products and combined clear introduction trailers from our own domain with personalised product-placement segments in cooperations with various gaming focussed content creators to reach our widely spread target audience.
Distilling the essence of Digital Product Design
Over the timespan of roughly a year Zack.ai managed to go from the first ideation phase past MVP testing and beta phase to a regularly iterated live product loved by millions of users.

Unfortunately, the concept of a delayed monetization through big user/usage data didn't develop enough traction — leading to closure of the complete user coaching oriented B2C section to cut costs and re-focus on the companies B2B business with its products Shadow.gg (nieche market of professional esports coaching) and BAYES (cash cow of analysing and selling game betting odds).

My 3+ years working with Dojo Madness have been filled to the brim with learnings about both, what makes a successful product go from 0 to 1 but also that well thought out financial concepts still is the key to enable long term product and business success.

The final year of creating Zack was condensing all this to cliff notes for a better understanding on how to balance user- & market needs with business requirements through not only well crafted UI/UX and product design but also company-wide ideation, alignment & cooperation.
Beni was one of those rare designers who really understood how to work with tech.
He had a strong product mindset, always thinking a few steps ahead and making sure design decisions made sense for both users and implementation.

Working with him was smooth — he was well-organized, open to feedback, and focused on finding solutions rather than getting stuck on problems. Plus, he brought a really positive and supportive energy to the team, which made a big difference in day-to-day collaboration.
Beni played a key role at Dojo Madness, both for the team and the company. He took full responsibility for the design team and really managed to support both the people and the business side.

I always appreciated how independent and reliable he was—he kept things running smoothly while also bringing in fresh ideas for new projects and branding. On top of that, Beni was super easy to work with: clear in communication, humble, and always supportive.
I had the pleasure of working closely with Beni for 3+ years. I relied on his attention to detail and breadth of Product Design expertise to guide me and our team to success.

He is a natural leader who easily fosters strong relationships with stakeholders, peers and team members. During challenges, he was a source of stability and certainty due to his solution-oriented attitude.
His clear communication and empathic mentoring approach allowed me to evolve in my role as a Product Designer.
Beni had a big impact on my growth as a designer. He always took the time to give thoughtful feedback and created a space where I felt safe to learn, try things out, and ask questions.
What stood out most was how he led by example — staying calm under pressure, thinking through problems with care, and always keeping the bigger picture in mind.
He was approachable, supportive, and genuinely cared about helping others grow.
No matter if you want to talk about your current / future product, problems to solve or just want to discuss design philosophy —
Just book a quick 15 minute call and I will curiously unpack your story to see where our potential overlap might take us.

/cs-wefox.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/cs-wefox.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
h4: Challenge: Design & handovers were inconsistent and hard to scale
Solution: CI manual & reusable component system
Challenge: Mismatched icon styles and inconsistent UI patterns
Solution: visual system foundation & Icon redesign
Challenge: Confusing layouts and unclear value
Challenge: Extending UX beyond mobile while building brand credibility
Solution: Responsive website redesign & marketing support
p: WeFox is an insurtech simplifying digital insurances to empower users with smarter, more transparent coverage solutions.
Cara Care develops a SaaS medical App to improve the quality of life for people with digestive issues like IBS, IBD and others.
Introduced Sketch and founded the in-house design team, replacing outsourced workflows
Mentored a Junior Designer, improving consistency, confidence and delivery speed
Mentored Junior Designer, improving consistency, confidence & delivery speed
Built a design system & CI manual, unifying product & brand across all media to a more premium appeal
End-to-end product design for three coaching products across web, mobile & desktop platforms
Improved information architexture with UX audits, hierarchy tweaks, and competitor analysis insights
Redesigned key app screens to boost clarity, trust and user perception in 4 months
Redesigned key app screens to boost clarity, trust & user perception
Extended product to web by redesigning the Website including new User-dashboards
Extended product to web by redesigning the Website including User-dashboards
Supported marketing with print, merch, and guidance for a nationwide TV ad campaign
Supported marketing with print, merch & guidance for a national TV campaign
When I joined WeFox, the product lacked a clear UX structure, visual coherence & polish and internal design ownership.
Over the course of 4 months, I led a comprehensive redesign of the core app experience, introduced the first design system + CI manual and helped extend the product from mobile to web.
These efforts elevated user trust & clarity and laid the foundation for long-term design scalability with a measurable boost in app store perception.
Over the course of 4 months, I led a comprehensive redesign of the core app experience, introduced the first design system & CI manual and helped extend the product from mobile to web.
Establishing the company’s first design system and CI guide.

With no basic design language in place, product design with multiple designers and handoffs to developers & external contractors were inefficient and error-prone. Brand communication varied wildly between channels.

I documented core design principles, components and brand visuals into a Corporate Design manual. This resource improved consistency across touchpoints and became the foundation for future internal & external design efforts.
Creating a scalable UI system to match the brand’s premium positioning.
informed Decisions towards Product Market Fit

The icon set was fragmented, visually clunky and communicated the opposite of the company’s high-quality, service-first positioning. There was also no shared library or style guide for the team or external partners.

With several designers working on different verticals with varying data & feature complexity, maintaining a unified design language & code base was a constant balancing act. Each game required rapid iteration and app-specific tweaks, but still had to feel like “SUMO.”

I redesigned necessary icons using a sharp, outline-based style with selective color accents. This visual logic became the basis of a more premium-feeling interface that aligned across product and marketing channels.
Clarifying the core user experience across screens that lacked structure and hierarchy.
Designing consistent user value despite fragmented & inconsistent data sources

The original app UI was visually noisy, structurally inconsistent and lacked visual guidance. Key user flows like the home screen, insurance overview and detail screens weren’t communicating trust or value, especially for first-time users.

The original app UI was noisy, structurally inconsistent and lacked visual guidance. Key user flows like the home screen, insurance overview and detail screens weren’t communicating trust or value, especially for first-time users.

I restructured key screens with a more clear and actionable information architecture to highlight spending insights, contract statuses and service options. Card-based layouts and a clearer hierarchy helped guide users through critical tasks like uploading insurance documents or viewing contract details.

I restructured key screens with a more clear and actionable information architecture to highlight spending insights, contract statuses and service options. Card-based layouts and a clear hierarchy helped guide users through critical tasks like uploading documents or viewing contract details.
Bringing the product to desktop and supporting brand storytelling.
From News-Feed to platform specific use-case split

The original product experience existed only on mobile. As the product matured, the team needed a responsive web experience and a new landing page that could communicate value to both users and potential investors.

Introducing a subscription-based model was uncommon at the time, especially in game companion apps. On top of that, our core value depended on third-party data sources that occasionally failed or changed.

I overhauled the core website, including dashboards for logged-in users and collaborated on guidance & visual assets for WeFox’s first major TV commercial, print campaigns and promotional merchandise to increase apation rate.

We designed upgrade flows that clearly communicated added value, using tiered features like ad removal, in-depth analytics, and custom coaching. When data outages occurred, we prioritized transparency and timely communication of details & expected duration to maintain user trust.
Distilling the essence of Digital Product Design
I moved WeFox from an outsourced, visually inconsistent product to a design-focussed brand with scalable foundations — all within just a few months.
The redesign improved UX clarity across key screens, boosted user trust in a sensitive service context, and enabled faster iteration through reusable design components.

With the new design system, icon language, and CI manual in place, the team could confidently scale across web, print, and external collaborations — while the app’s improved usability and visual polish contributed to a noticeable increase in user satisfaction and store ratings.
This project was a pivotal moment shifting from solving screens to shaping systems to find the balance between execution & future proofing.
The experience shaped how I approach rapid impact, team enablement, and cross-disciplinary collaboration to this day.
No matter if you want to talk about your current / future product, problems to solve or just want to discuss design philosophy —
Just book a quick 15 minute call and I will curiously unpack your story to see where our potential overlap might take us.

/cs-sumo.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/cs-sumo.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
h2: The SUMO family — companion apps for game coaching
h4: Challenge: Different game developers, different data
Solution: Unified experience via flexible feature design framework
Solution: Shared design system & active collaboration culture
Challenge: Delivering insights from sparse or delayed data
Solution: Creative coaching logic & progressive disclosure
Challenge: Subscription fatigue & external service dependency
Solution: Transparent upsells & layered premium offering
Challenge: Young users, high standards, short attention spans
p: Dojo Madness is a gaming startup harnessing big data to build tools & services that help players to master their favorite games.
Cara Care develops a SaaS medical App to improve the quality of life for people with digestive issues like IBS, IBD and others.
Scaled and led a team of 5 senior designers & 1 freelancer across multiple product lines
End-to-end Product design for 3 products, balancing brand cohesion with game-specific user needs across mobile, web & desktop platforms
End-to-end product design for three coaching products across web, mobile & desktop platforms
Creation & maintenance of a design system improving visual consistency and streamlining development across all SUMO verticals
Launched OVERSUMO from concept to monetization, growing it to 5M+ users
Designed high-converting in-app upgrade flows and teaser surfaces, accelerating revenue growth and improving user adoption
Supported marketing, sales & content teams in print, merchandise, web & motion needs
SUMO became a family of mobile coaching apps helping players improve in competitive titles like LoL, Overwatch, and DOTA 2.
By adapting our core experience to multiple games with very different data landscapes, we built a flexible system that users trusted and returned to.
The monetization model, subscription-based and relatively new to this space at the time, was well received by our core audience and helped fund future product pivots.
Designing consistent user value despite fragmented and inconsistent data sources
Designing consistent user value despite fragmented & inconsistent data sources

Each game vertical posed its own data constraints: League of Legends offered a robust API, Overwatch required scraping Blizzard’s public player profiles and Dota 2 provided only limited stats. These differences made it hard to offer consistent, timely and granular insights across apps.

To tackle this issue, we created a modular design system & feature architecture that allowed us to adapt each app’s depth and specificity while maintaining a coherent visual and functional experience. This let us scale the SUMO brand without sacrificing usability or trust.
Maintaining cohesion across product lines while moving fast with a growing team.
informed Decisions towards Product Market Fit

With several designers working on different verticals with varying complexity, maintaining a unified design language & code base was a constant balancing act. Each game required rapid iteration and app-specific tweaks, but still had to feel like “SUMO.”

With several designers working on different verticals with varying data & feature complexity, maintaining a unified design language & code base was a constant balancing act. Each game required rapid iteration and app-specific tweaks, but still had to feel like “SUMO.”

We invested in a central design system and held regular design reviews, cross-vertical sparring sessions and check-ins with dev leads. This helped us evolve shared patterns, find blind spots early, and accelerate implementation while keeping quality high.
Turning incomplete data into coaching users could trust and act on.

Due to the functionality on API-supported SUMO verticals, players expected real-time feedback and detailed coaching in each app. But in some cases we only had delayed or incomplete data to work with due to dependencies on external data sources. This mismatch threatened product experience, user trust and engagement.

We combined player-specific stats with scraped community knowledge and aggregated performance patterns to offer actionable tips. By using progressive disclosure and contextual explanations, we kept users informed and engaged even when full data wasn’t available.
Introducing a new business model without losing credibility or loyalty.
From News-Feed to platform specific use-case split

Introducing a subscription-based model was uncommon at the time, especially in companion apps for gaming. On top of that, our core value depended on third-party data sources that occasionally failed or changed.

Introducing a subscription-based model was uncommon at the time, especially in game companion apps. On top of that, our core value depended on third-party data sources that occasionally failed or changed.

We designed upgrade flows that clearly communicated added value, using tiered features like ad removal, in-depth analytics, and custom coaching. When data outages occurred, we prioritized transparency and timely user communication to maintain trust.

We designed upgrade flows that clearly communicated added value, using tiered features like ad removal, in-depth analytics, and custom coaching. When data outages occurred, we prioritized transparency and timely communication of details & expected duration to maintain user trust.
Building authentic engagement with a digitally native, high-expectation user base.
Building engagement with a digitally native, high-expectation user base.

The SUMO audience was tech-savvy and skeptical of traditional marketing. Reaching them for sufficient user growth required more than flashy visuals. We needed to speak their language, prove our value quickly and offer direct, two-way communication on their terms.

The complexity of the markets prescription / reimbursement system had to be tackled through a well structured and extensively guiding customer journey that enables multiple entry points (doctor referrals, self-managed, etc.) and confidently guides the user with supportively chunked steps, reminders  & info material.

As part of the marketing efforts, we launched a dedicated product landing page, including a short explanation video and targeted influencer placements to introduce OVERSUMO in a tone and style that resonated with the gaming community.

To foster user trust and get a channel for direct, unfiltered user feedback, we created a dedicated subreddit for announcements, feedback and support — enabling async, transparent communication through upvoting and community replies.
Together, these efforts turned marketing into an extension of the product experience and gave users a sense of ownership in the app’s evolution.
Distilling the essence of Digital Product Design
SUMO grew into a widely recognized family of coaching apps that empowered competitive gamers to understand and improve their performance through data-driven insights.
Across LOLSUMO, OVERSUMO, and DOTASUMO, we reached over 13 million downloads, maintained an average store rating of 4.5 stars, and achieved a five-digit monthly recurring revenue.
The successful monetization of SUMO products laid the foundation for the company’s shift toward a broader platform strategy thinking well beyond the original apps.
Leading AND actively participating in design across the SUMO family taught me to balance consistency with flexibility, craft with systems, and team support with product focus.
Particularly my two-sided mentoring approach on equal footing allowed me to significantly improve my coaching style and grow together as a team.
I also saw firsthand how the lack of direct control over data sources can drasticly challenge UX, but also open the door to creative, out of the box solutions.
Beni was one of those rare designers who really understood how to work with tech.
He had a strong product mindset, always thinking a few steps ahead and making sure design decisions made sense for both users and implementation.

Working with him was smooth — he was well-organized, open to feedback, and focused on finding solutions rather than getting stuck on problems. Plus, he brought a really positive and supportive energy to the team, which made a big difference in day-to-day collaboration.
Beni played a key role at Dojo Madness, both for the team and the company. He took full responsibility for the design team and really managed to support both the people and the business side.

I always appreciated how independent and reliable he was—he kept things running smoothly while also bringing in fresh ideas for new projects and branding. On top of that, Beni was super easy to work with: clear in communication, humble, and always supportive.
I had the pleasure of working closely with Beni for 3+ years. I relied on his attention to detail and breadth of Product Design expertise to guide me and our team to success.

He is a natural leader who easily fosters strong relationships with stakeholders, peers and team members. During challenges, he was a source of stability and certainty due to his solution-oriented attitude.
His clear communication and empathic mentoring approach allowed me to evolve in my role as a Product Designer.
Beni had a big impact on my growth as a designer. He always took the time to give thoughtful feedback and created a space where I felt safe to learn, try things out, and ask questions.
What stood out most was how he led by example — staying calm under pressure, thinking through problems with care, and always keeping the bigger picture in mind.
He was approachable, supportive, and genuinely cared about helping others grow.
No matter if you want to talk about your current / future product, problems to solve or just want to discuss design philosophy —
Just book a quick 15 minute call and I will curiously unpack your story to see where our potential overlap might take us.

/cs-caracare.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/cs-caracare.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
h2: The mobile app for  Digital Gastrointestinal Therapeutics
h6: Active NDA: Case study contains vague details to protect client's IP.
p: IC Design Lead & Design Team Lead
Cara Care develops a SaaS medical App to improve the quality of life for people with digestive issues like IBS, IBD and others.
Conducted job interviews, hired team members
Design team organization, planning & process optimization
End-to-end product design for various features
Rebranding & alignment to business & user needs
Creation & maintenance of an Design System
Creation & maintenance of a multi-purpose Design System
Support for marketing, sales & content via print, merchandise, web & motion design
Support for marketing, sales & content via print, merch, web & motion
Over the 4+ years of the cooperation Cara Care made the shift from an MVP to a holistic, well-selling product Brand with significant increase in users, adaption rate and positive treatment results.

It not only helped patients previously stuck in the outdated medical system regaining their health & quality of life but also achieved an exit to Bayer AG (German multinational pharmaceutical and biotechnology company).

A baseline branding, understanding of market situation & target group were given but lacked the story & finesse to connect with the users on the particularly delicate & personal topic of digestive health.
The initial product was in the shape of a MVP with a minimal feature set and various UI / UX struggles due to the lack of a clear Vision, Corporate Identity & Design System.
Marketing efforts & customer journey existed in rough outlines but suffered under the indirect & complex payment approach of reimbursement via users’ health insurances.

In conclusion these typical loose ends of early stage startups were meant to be adressed & realligned holistically towards a more user centered approach that creatively handles the complex market & payment situation within the german healthcare system.

From an informed vision to a supporting corporate identity, design principles for fitting product features & user journey, ending in a retention strategy after the initial user treatment journey has ended.
Insights for informed Decisions towards Product Market Fit
informed Decisions towards Product Market Fit
Through interviews with Users and Key Stakeholders (both inhouse & external advisors) we became painfully aware of:
the target group variety in terms of both basic criteria (age & gender) and  topic specifics (e.g. pre-existing illnesses, allergies & intolerances)
the collectively shared struggles of helplessness, shame and isolation with these conditions
the fact-driven, time restricted & non-compassionate healthcare system and it’s required steps for anamnesis, contraindications and exclusions
the need for a personalised, supportive approach based on individual patients’ set & setting (allergies, diseases, mental state, dedication etc.)

Since the healthcare market for medical treatment via software (DiGA’s) was still in its baby shoes, chances for new approaches were arising but had to be carefully steered through various slow buerocratic hurdles (medical studies, insurance coverage, audits ...)

Since the healthcare market for medical treatment via software (DiGA’s) was still in its baby shoes, chances for new approaches were arising but had to be carefully steered through various slow buerocratic hurdles (medical studies, insurance coverage, audits...)

Since the healthcare market for medical treatment via software (DiGA’s) was still in its baby shoes, chances for new approaches were arising but had to be carefully steered through various slow buerocratic hurdles (medical studies, insurance coverage, audits, etc...)
With fresh clarity on patient needs, market challenges and a new path for opportunities the adjustments needed for the company vision became clear:
bridge the gap between users and healthcare service requirements
create awareness, practice compassion, stay discrete and trustworthy
adjust the product from a static tool to a flexible companion
rethink the customer journey to a holistic approach from first contact to retention after treatment success
Defining the Nieche & filling it with Character

To save time we hired an external agency to support the shift from old brand basics to holistic corporate identity. I coordinated and streamlined these efforts to keep alignment while working out the product vision adjustments in parallel.

The Corporate Philosophy had to fill the gap in the user & market need between a general physician and a close friend in order to fulfill both key virtues of discretion & professionalism but also trustworthiness & empathy.

The Corporate Philosophy had to fill the gap in the user & market need between a general physician and a close friend in order to fulfill both key virtues of professionalism &  discretion but also trustworthiness & empathy.

The Corporate Philosophy had to fill the gap in the user & market need between a general physician and a close friend in order to fulfill both key virtues of professionalism & discretion but also trustworthiness & empathy.

In order to achieve a more human and empathic approach the Corporate Design needed to shift to an easily “digestible” style with high general appeal through organic shapes, desaturated natural colors, extended usage of white space and friendly illustrations mixed with authentic imagery.

Medical credibility on the other hand was maintained through a well balanced Corporate Communication  & Language with well chunked bits of accurate & actionable information that is free from medical jargon, mixed with a supportive & reassuring tonality that is unencumbered by taboos.
From DYI-Tools to holistic Treatment Companion

The product vision and feature set had to adapt from a plain analytical food & stool tracker tool to a companion that augments the traditional medical treatments: It explains, consults, and offers practical help. With the data it generates, it can also help doctors diagnose a users conditions more easily.

This lead to the development of a guided program, personalised to the specific user needs & conditions (time quota, diseases, allergies, etc) as the main feature. The existing tracking features were offered in an additional tool-kit, combined with a powerful correlation analysis to help the user understand this data and weaved into the main program where supportive.
Supporting the new Company & Product Vision various changes were rolled out in feature versions to keep the app usable and the user adaption cost as low as possible. Some of the bigger adjustments:
Home Screen became a hub for next steps in the guided program, latest tracking insights, nutritional suggestions and general reminders

Information architecture and User Flow of the Guided program was well chunked into various media types & occasional quizzes alligned through a weekly content cadence with monthly recurring personalisation check-ins
required regular medical check-ins were modularized and combined with a evaluation screen showing the users treatment progress and enabling quick handover to the physician of choice
Extensive tracking tools was simplified and linked to correlation analysis with a simple yet understandable evaluation system
To improve short term retention and joy of use the general design language was tranlated into the UI & UX and mixed with various small but smart interactions & micro-animations.
Solving Market Problems through extensive Multichannel Guidance

The complexity of the markets prescription/reimbursement system had to be tackled through a well structured and extensively guiding customer journey that enables multiple entry points (doctor referrals, self-managed, etc.) and confidently guides the user with supportively chunked steps, reminders  & info material.

The complexity of the markets prescription / reimbursement system had to be tackled through a well structured and extensively guiding customer journey that enables multiple entry points (doctor referrals, self-managed, etc.) and confidently guides the user with supportively chunked steps, reminders  & info material.
a completely overhauled Website with base informations for various user situations, pre-funneled via Typeform surveys
extensive use of various info materials from leaflets to checklists and introduction letters for physicians
various reach-out channels from social media to email campaigns and publications in magazines with both medical and lifestyle focus
active reach-outs to specialised physicians  for faster prescription & recommendation routes via sales
From kickstart through growing pains to exit

Cara Care made the shift from an MVP to a holistic, well-selling product Brand with significant increase in users, adaption rate & positive treatment results. It not only helped patients previously stuck in the outdated medical system regaining their health & quality of life but also achieved an exit to Bayer AG (German multinational pharmaceutical & biotechnology company).

Over the 4+ years of shifting from MVP to well-selling product I was able to witness lots of various growing pains and requirement shifts throughout the whole company. I filled various roles and was able to utilize & refine a lot of my professional skills & passions, learning so much on the way.
Ultimately this cooperation helped me to really understand:
the industry challenges for digital product companies
the importance of proper adaption strategies vs. quick-fixes (80/20 rule)
the shared value in emphasizing & solving this recurring struggle in a win-win manner that drives progression rather than slowing down the momentum.

Working with Beni at Cara Care was a real privilege. He brings a rare blend of broad design skills — spanning UI/UX, motion, web, and print — and a deeply human-centered product mindset. He combines structure, curiosity, and critical thinking to create meaningful, user-focused solutions.
His communication is clear and adaptive, and he collaborates seamlessly across teams and disciplines. Above all, he’s humble, creative, and consistently elevates the people and products around him.

I would like to note Beni’s leadership qualities. She always knows how to find a common language with the team and establish proper communication with each team member. At the same time, he keeps the focus on business goals and does everything to ensure that the team achieves them.
His experience as a designer further helps her evaluate a product's user experience and identify many opportunities to improve that experience.
Beni is a great design lead — a sharp critical thinker with a strong sense for product and user experience.
He combines a holistic view with structured problem-solving and clear, adaptive communication that works with all team members.
Whether mentoring others or driving work hands-on, Beni brings curiosity, accountability, and a calm, collaborative presence that elevates the team around him.
No matter if you want to talk about your current / future product, problems to solve or just want to discuss design philosophy —
Just book a quick 15 minute call and I will curiously unpack your story to see where our potential overlap might take us.

/about.title: Benjamin Erxleben - Digital Product Designer
/about.description: I’m a freelance Digital Product Designer and Design Multidisciplinarian helping growing startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
h3: Adaptability is the core driver in both design & life.
Overcoming growing pains, focus drain & trade-offs
Design Chameleon adapting to your needs
Overcoming Growing pains, Focus drain & trade-offs
p: Seasoned Design Chameleon — living between contrasts, driven by curiosity — continuously adapting & reshaping my perception of life at the intersection of creativity & research.
The faster you are able to adapt, the more you can design process and outcome.
Adaptability struggles during growth slow key factors of product scaling and focus
Picking the skills & time frame YOU need to minimize pain and maximize outcome.
A fresh boost & perspective to strengthen both your product & my career diversity.
The faster you are able to adapt, the more you can design process & outcome.
Adaptability struggles during growth slow key factors of product scaling & focus.
Picking skills & time drame YOU need to minimize pain and maximize outcome.
What I learned along the way
My Goal: Seamless adaptation to your individual tool chain
IC Lead  |  Mentoring  |  Design Team Lead
Need someone who can rally the design troops and keep the creative vision sharp?
I step in as an individual contributor (IC) or team lead to help shape direction, guide designers, and make sure no good idea gets lost in the Slack abyss.

I support teams hands-on, whether they need mentorship, a strong creative lead, or someone to help scale design processes as the product grows. Think of me as a mix of coach, captain, and contributor—without the drama.
Every great product starts with knowing who it’s for and why it matters.

I dig into user needs, business goals, and market trends to uncover real opportunities (not just guesses). From there, it’s all about generating & shaping ideas — sketches, flows, napkin scribbles — until we land on a concept worth building. It’s half investigation, half imagination, and fully essential.
Your brand is more than just a logo — it’s how people feel when they interact with your company.

I help craft visual identities that actually say something, from the colors and typography to the overall vibe. Whether you’re starting fresh or refreshing a tired look, I’ll create a system that works across all channels and tells your story consistently. One part strategy, one part style, all heart.
From slick mobile apps to robust web platforms, I design digital products that are easy to use, beautiful to look at, and built to solve real problems.
This includes UX (how it works) and UI (how it looks) — so your users don’t have to guess, click twice, or scream into a pillow.
I balance user needs with business goals to create a holistic little something that is functional and delightful. No unnecessary bells and whistles — unless they’re fun to use.
Sometimes your product needs more than pixels on a screen.
I create visual designs for digital campaigns and traditional print — whether it’s a killer keynote deck, a social ad, or a good old-fashioned brochure. Everything stays on-brand and on-point.
It’s design that speaks the same language everywhere your audience hangs out.
Why guess what works when you can test it?
I build interactive prototypes that bring ideas to life before they’re fully developed. Then we run them by real users to see what clicks (and what doesn’t).
This helps us validate concepts early, catch issues fast, and avoid costly “oops” moments down the line. Think of it as quality control for your big idea.
Already have a product but not sure if the design’s pulling its weight?

I do thorough design audits — checking usability, accessibility, consistency, and overall experience. I also help test for edge cases, spot visual bugs, and suggest improvements that’ll make your product smoother, smarter, and more lovable.
It’s like a check-up for your products design health.
Whether you're stuck on a specific challenge or just need fresh eyes on your product, I offer consultations tailored to your needs.
I also run hands-on workshops to align teams, explore ideas, or kickstart projects. No buzzwords, no post-it marathons — just collaborative sessions with real outcomes.
Bring your team, your questions, and maybe some laughs for in between.
Bachelor of Arts  in Interface Design
Prof. Training as Designer  for Digital & Print Media
IC Design Lead & Design Team Lead

My life has always been shaped by the vast contrasts surrounding me — fueling my everlasting curiosity for understanding and driving persistent adaptation through readjustment to my core values, living conditions, and passions.

Born in Berlin (Germany) as an only child within a patchwork family of six half-siblings — spread across different ages, places and levels of connection — adaptability was ingrained in me from the very beginning.

As the child of two professional classical musicians with family traditions rooted in artistic crafts — I quickly squirmed out of imposed piano lessons and toward my own chosen field of passion and curiosity — understanding technology.
After I eventually disassembled (and occasionally even fixed) every electronic device at home, I began hacking & improving my most used & beloved ones: game consoles.
Shifting from Hardware to Software while building my first websites around the age of 14, I soon discovered a new field to dive into and scratch my constant itch of curiosity — the human mind.

So I threw myself into the vast oceans of Psychology & Philosophy, realizing that my inner curiosity & drive wouldn’t be fulfilled by gaining understanding & logic alone, but by also utilizing my artistic heritage to create. This inevitably led me to my profession of passion: Design.

I explored the vast variety within the field and eventually found my sweet spot in Digital Product Design — a discipline combining high demand for analytical thinking with creative aspects of crafting diverse outputs & support materials, all filtered through the translation layers of psychology & (brand) philosophy.
No matter if you want to talk about your current / future product, problems to solve or just want to discuss design philosophy —
Just book a quick 15 minute call and I will curiously unpack your story to see where our potential overlap might take us.

p: Seasoned Product Designer helping startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
Seasoned Product Designerhelping startups & companies navigate the challenges of scaling design via flexible, on-demand expertise across all typical design needs & product stages.
Turning growing pains into a Win-Win
Serving individual Design-Needs to minimize pain & maximize outcome
I seamlessly integrate to deliver precise execution, strategic guidance or mentoring based on your individual requirements — minimizing time, budget & cultural strain.
My approach fosters sustainable impact, reduces growing pains and builds a synergetic partnership both parties will value and thrive in.
What I bring to the table
Some of the Skills I’ve acquired over the years:

No matter where your product stands, I’ve been there. From ideation & hypothesis testing to MVP creation, monetization, launch and continuous feature improvement — I help to drive impact at every stage.

I find the right balance between rapid impact and long-term evolution based on your individual needs. With solid experience in both fast-paced agencies and design-driven startups, I’m able to deliver both.
Proficiency in both Roles enables me to understand & improve your Challenges holistically.
Proficiency in both Roles enables me to understand and improve your Challenges.
As Design Team & IC Lead and advocate of the Getting-Things-Done Methodology I learned to optimize the balance between Interaction and Execution time.
I had the pleasure of working closely with Beni for 3+ years. I relied on his attention to detail and breadth of Product Design expertise to guide me and our team to success.

He is a natural leader who easily fosters strong relationships with stakeholders, peers and team members. During challenges, he was a source of stability and certainty due to his solution-oriented attitude.
His clear communication and empathic mentoring approach allowed me to evolve in my role as a Product Designer.

Beni was a real asset to our team. He combined strong UX/UI skills with a sharp eye for design systems and consistently delivered high-quality work. What stood out was his strategical thinking and bringing user needs into conversations.

His calm, thoughtful presence helped the team stay focused and make progress. He brought a holistic, human-centered approach and communicated clearly across teams—making him not only a reliable contributor but also a supportive collaborator and mentor.
Beni played a key role at Dojo Madness, both for the team and the company. He took full responsibility for the design team and really managed to support both the people and the business side.

I always appreciated how independent and reliable he was—he kept things running smoothly while also bringing in fresh ideas for new projects and branding. On top of that, Beni was super easy to work with: clear in communication, humble, and always supportive.

Working with Beni at Cara Care was a real privilege. He brings a rare blend of broad design skills — spanning UI/UX, motion, web, and print — and a deeply human-centered product mindset. He combines structure, curiosity, and critical thinking to create meaningful, user-focused solutions.
His communication is clear and adaptive, and he collaborates seamlessly across teams and disciplines. Above all, he’s humble, creative, and consistently elevates the people and products around him.

What I’ve always loved about working with Beni for over a year is the excellent teamwork: clear communication, strong self-organization and a consistent openness to giving and receiving feedback which makes him the perfect design sparring partner.

Beni has a great eye for detail without losing sight of the big picture. He enriches every team with his broad understanding of user-centered design and business expertise—benefiting all team members, regardless of their profession.
Beni is a great design lead — a sharp critical thinker with a strong sense for product and user experience.
He combines a holistic view with structured problem-solving and clear, adaptive communication that works with all team members.
Whether mentoring others or driving work hands-on, Beni brings curiosity, accountability, and a calm, collaborative presence that elevates the team around him.
Craft and teamwork like no other. Have fun working with him! — Or in his own words: "Handwerk und Teamwork, die ihresgleichen suchen. Viel Spaß mit ihm! Ein Lächeln – Robert"

* Robert belongs to the rare breed of people who deliberately do things differently. When asked for a testimonial, his answer was simple: "Long texts? Nobody reads those anymore!"Which, honestly, says it all about our shared overlap in values — we both believe in getting to the point to make things meaningful.

Whether you invest your precious time & funds in employing a seasoned all-rounder who is hard to sustain over the long term or build a team of specialists that requires constant alignment and utilization to stay cost effective — both paths come with trade-offs.

When time is of the essence, working with me gives you immediate access to an experienced designer and allows you to focus on what’s important — developing or enhancing your product, with flexibility and risk-free.
It will feel like working with a regular employee to you.

I seamlessly integrate with your team(s), adapt to your tools and am available during regular business hours. Due to my international client baseI I am most proficient working remotely but able to offer limited in-house support depending on location and time frame.
Due to my minimum project size / duration my schedule usually is quite stuffed to easily fit in lengthy consultation processes, though.
But I'd suggest a quick sync-call to create more clarity towards potential opportunities and a synergetic outlook for both sides.
